skill.name: csharp-pro
skill.description: Guidance for modern C# and .NET development, including refactoring, architecture, async code, performance work, and comprehensive testing.
skill.summary: Modern C# and .NET guidance for refactoring, architecture, async code, testing, and performance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and no confirmed dangerous behavior was found. The backtick finding at line 26 is Markdown formatting for a local resource path, and the weak cryptography findings at lines 3 and 34 are ordinary prose about C# and SOLID design.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Formatting
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md line 26. The line only references `resources/implementation-playbook.md` as a Markdown-formatted local resource path and does not execute a command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is plain Markdown instruction text. No shell syntax, interpreter directive, or executable script content is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Term in Description
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at SKILL.md line 3. The line is part of the skill description for modern C# development and contains no cryptographic algorithm or implementation guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The source line is descriptive metadata about C# records and pattern matching. There is no code, algorithm name, or security-sensitive cryptographic recommendation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: SOLID Design Term
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at SKILL.md line 34. The line says SOLID principles and design patterns in C#, which is software design guidance and not cryptographic advice.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The term SOLID refers to object-oriented design principles in this context. The surrounding focus areas contain no cryptographic code or unsafe algorithm usage.

content.value_statement: C# teams often need help applying modern language features without weakening architecture or tests. This skill guides refactoring, performance work, async design, and enterprise .NET implementation.
actual_capabilities: Guides use of records, pattern matching, nullable reference types, and modern C# syntax.
Reviews C# architecture for SOLID principles, composition, and enterprise design patterns.
Plans ASP.NET Core, Entity Framework, Blazor, and microservice implementation work.
Improves async and concurrent code using async/await and Task Parallel Library patterns.
Recommends unit testing approaches with xUnit, NUnit, Moq, and FluentAssertions.
Suggests performance improvements using spans, memory-aware types, and BenchmarkDotNet.
limitations: It provides guidance and generated code, but it does not run .NET builds by itself.
It depends on project context for framework versions, target runtime, and coding standards.
It cannot verify production performance without benchmarks or runtime telemetry.
It is not a substitute for security review of authentication, authorization, or data handling.

use_cases.title: Refactor Legacy C# Services
use_cases.description: Modernize service code with nullable reference types, records, cleaner error handling, and focused tests.

use_cases.title: Design Enterprise .NET Features
use_cases.description: Plan ASP.NET Core, Entity Framework, and microservice components with maintainable architecture.

use_cases.title: Improve Test Coverage
use_cases.description: Create unit test strategies, mocking boundaries, and meaningful assertions for existing C# code.

prompt_templates.title: Review a C# Class
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a focused code sample
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this C# class for readability, nullable reference safety, and simple refactoring opportunities. Explain each recommendation.

prompt_templates.title: Modernize a .NET Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate refactoring with current C# features
prompt_templates.prompt: Modernize this .NET component using records, pattern matching, and async best practices. Keep public behavior unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Design Tests for a Service
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate testing plan for application code
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a unit testing plan for this C# service using xUnit and Moq. Include key test cases, boundaries, and assertion goals.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize an Async Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this async C# workflow for blocking calls, allocation pressure, cancellation handling, and throughput risks. Propose measurable improvements.

output_examples.input: Review a C# API handler that mixes validation, database access, and response mapping.
output_examples.output: A structured review that separates validation, persistence, mapping, and error handling, with recommended tests for each responsibility.

output_examples.input: Improve an async service method that sometimes blocks under load.
output_examples.output: A concurrency analysis covering blocking calls, cancellation tokens, exception flow, and benchmark ideas for validating the improvement.

output_examples.input: Plan tests for a repository-backed business service.
output_examples.output: A focused test plan with mocked dependencies, success and failure cases, edge conditions, and assertion guidance.

best_practices: Provide target framework, C# language version, and relevant project conventions.
Share focused code samples with expected behavior and current failing tests when available.
Ask for performance changes with measurable goals and representative workload details.
anti_patterns: Requesting broad rewrites without explaining compatibility requirements or public contracts.
Applying advanced C# syntax when simpler code would be clearer for the team.
Optimizing allocations or concurrency before defining benchmarks or observed bottlenecks.

faq.question: Does this skill support modern C# features?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on records, pattern matching, nullable reference types, async/await, and related modern .NET practices.

faq.question: Can it help with ASP.NET Core projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide API design, service boundaries, dependency injection, Entity Framework usage, and testing patterns.

faq.question: Can it generate unit tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It can propose tests using xUnit, NUnit, Moq, and FluentAssertions based on the code you provide.

faq.question: Does it run benchmarks automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can suggest BenchmarkDotNet scenarios and interpretation steps, but execution depends on your local environment.

faq.question: Is it useful for legacy C# code?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest incremental refactors that preserve behavior while improving safety, readability, and testability.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the target framework, language version, relevant code, expected behavior, and any tests or performance symptoms.
